_meta.title: KCPE Mathematics 2023 Kenya - V2 import package
notes: Built against the XamPreps V2 import contract.
This package uses stable local refs for sections, instruction groups, items, interactions, marking rules, and model answers.
A script should resolve local refs to real Firestore IDs in the required write order.
No auto-generated IDs or timestamps are included.

exam.title: KCPE Mathematics 2023
exam.subject: Mathematics
exam.overallInstructions: You have been given this question paper and a separate answer sheet. The question paper contains 50 questions. Answer all questions. For each of the questions 1-50, four choices are given. Choose the correct answer.

sections.title: Section A
sections.sharedInstructions: Questions 1 to 50 carry one mark each.

instructionGroups.instructionsMarkdown: For each question, choose the correct answer from options A, B, C and D.

items.stemMarkdown: What is three million forty eight thousand two hundred and fifty seven written in numerals?
What is the place value of 7 in the number 9843.071?

What is the next number in the pattern shown below?

3/4, 1 1/4, 1 3/4, 2 1/4, ...
What is 3/8 as a percentage?
Charity cycled from home to school a distance of 12 kilometres. She started the journey at 7.20 a.m and arrived at 8.00 a.m. What was her speed in metres per second?

The figure below shows a trapezium UWXY. Line YX is parallel to line UW. The perpendicular distance between the parallel lines is 12 cm and line YX = 18 cm. The area of the trapezium is 252 cm². What is the length of line UW?

A teacher shared pencils among his learners. The girls received 2/3 of the pencils and the boys received 5/6 of the remainder. The teacher remained with 8 pencils. How many pencils did the teacher have before sharing?
A school received five thousand and forty two books. The books were delivered in two lorries. The first lorry had three thousand and sixteen books. How many books were in the second lorry?
What is the value of 1/2 of (2/3 − 1/6) ÷ 1 1/4 ?
Cylindrical tins each of diameter 20 cm and a height of 15 cm were packed in a carton. The carton measured 120 cm long, 80 cm wide and 30 cm high. How many tins were packed in the carton?
What is the value of (16.8 × 0.054) ÷ 0.072 ?
A scale drawing of a rectangular plot of land measures 3 cm by 2 cm. The scale used is 1:2000. What is the actual area of the plot in ares?
What is the value of x in the equation (2x/3) − (4x/9) = 12?
What is 567432 rounded off to the nearest ten thousands?
Three bells ring at intervals of 12 minutes, 24 minutes and 36 minutes. They rang together at 10.00 a.m. When was the last time they rang together before 10.00 a.m?
The area of a semicircle is 39.25 cm². What is the length of the diameter? (Take π = 3.14)
A pupil reported to a boarding school on 27th January, 2019. He went for a mid-term break on 18th March 2019. How many nights did he spend in school?
The area of a square plot of land is 0.0576 hectares. The plot was fenced all round using five strands of wire. What was the total length of the wire used?
A pipe has a diameter of 0.7 m and a length of 3 m. The outer surface of the pipe was painted. What was the area that was painted? (Take π = 22/7)
What is the value of [4n²(r + y) + ny] ÷ (r + y − n), when n = 2, r = 3 and y = 5?

A printing press produced 11,457 books on Monday. It produced 308 more books on Tuesday than on Monday. On Wednesday, it produced 4,796 less books than the total number produced both on Monday and Tuesday. How many books were produced in the three days?
A cylindrical container has a diameter of 56 cm and a height of 30 cm. The container is half filled with water. How many litres of water are in the container? (Take π = 22/7)
Construct triangle PQR such that line PQ = 10 cm and angle RPQ = angle RQP = 40°. Construct a circle that touches the vertices P, Q and R. What is the measure of the diameter of the circle?
A van is loaded with 120 cartons each containing 40 packets of salt. The mass of each empty carton is 500 g and that of each packet of salt is 750 g. What is the total mass of the load in kilograms?
Kelvin deposited sh 15,000 in a bank. The bank paid compound interest at the rate of 12% per annum. What was the total interest paid by the bank at the end of two years?
Ntimama had 40 cows and 55 goats in his farm. He sold 10% of the cows and increased the number of goats by 20%. What was the new number of animals in the farm?
The area of a right angled triangle is 54 cm². The length of one of the shorter sides is 12 cm. What is the perimeter of the triangle?

A saleslady earns a basic salary of sh 10,500 per month. She also earns a commission on sales above sh 100,000. In one month, she sold items worth sh 150,000 and earned a total of sh 18,000. What was the percentage commission?
A rectangular floor measures 7.5 m long and 2.5 m wide. The floor is covered with square tiles of side 0.5 m. How many tiles were used to cover the floor completely?
What is the value of 7(3² + 4) − 5 × 8 ÷ 2 + 6?
The following figure shows a triangle drawn inside a semicircle. What is the area of the shaded part in cm²? Take π = 22/7.
What is the square of 6 1/4?
A family uses 30 decilitres of milk everyday. How many litres of milk did they use during the months of February and March 2016?
A cuboid measures 6 cm long, 5 cm wide and 4 cm high. What is the total length of the edges?

The cash price of a sewing machine is sh 24,800. The hire purchase price is 15% more than the cash price. Joseph purchased the machine on hire purchase terms. He paid a deposit of sh 8,520 and ten equal monthly instalments. How much was the monthly instalment?
The average mass of 36 pupils is 45 kg. The average mass of the pupils together with their teacher is 46 kg. How much more is the mass of the teacher than the average mass of the pupils?
The marked price of a bag of beans was sh 4,500. A school bought eight bags of beans at a total cost of sh 34,400. What was the total discount allowed?

A trader bought 10 crates of soda at sh 600 per crate. He paid sh 30 per crate for transportation. Each crate of soda contained 24 bottles but ten bottles broke during transportation. He later sold each bottle of soda for sh 30. What percentage profit did the trader make?

A poultry farmer bought the following items: 2 chick feeders @ sh 85; 5 bags of chick mash @ sh 450; 4 bags of growers mash @ sh 650; 2 packets of chick formula for sh 150. She paid for the items using 6 one thousand shilling notes. How much balance did she get?
In the figure shown, lines RS and TU are parallel. Line VW is a transversal. Which one of the following statements is correct?

Juma bought a pair of trousers for sh r. He also bought a pair of shoes for twice the price of a pair of trousers and a shirt for half the price of the pair of trousers. He paid using two sh x notes. Which one of the following expressions represents the balance Juma got?
The following pie chart shows sales made by a farmer in a certain day. The value of the sales for maize was sh 880. What was the value of the sales for bananas?
Which one of the following statements is correct about all right angled triangles?
Eighteen men working at the same rate can complete a job in 30 days. How many more men, working at the same rate can complete the same job in 12 days?

The following are properties of quadrilaterals: (i) all sides are equal (ii) opposite angles are equal (iii) all angles are equal (iv) diagonals bisect each other. Which one of the properties is true for a rhombus?
Which one of the following statements is correct?
Construct triangle PQR such that line PQ = 8 cm, angle RPQ = 45° and angle PRQ = 75°. Construct a perpendicular line from point R to meet line PQ at M. What is the measure of line RM?

The following table shows a timetable for a certain aeroplane plying from Airport S to Airport V. A passenger arrived at Airport T at 1.30 p.m. How long did the passenger have to wait to depart from the airport?

Brenda has x books. Amina has thrice as many books as Brenda. Juma has 10 books less than the total number of books both Brenda and Amina have. They have a total of 86 books altogether. Which one of the following equations can be used to find the number of books Brenda has?
The bar graph below shows the amount of milk, in litres, a farmer got from his farm in one week. Which two days following each other in the week had the highest total production of milk?

value.label: D
value.text: The longest side is opposite the smallest angle

modelAnswerVersions.explanation: Three million is 3,000,000 and forty eight thousand two hundred and fifty seven is 48,257. Adding gives 3,048,257.
After the decimal point, the first digit is tenths (0), the second is hundredths (7), and the third is thousandths (1). So 7 is in the hundredths place.
Each term increases by 1/2: 3/4 to 1 1/4, then 1 3/4, then 2 1/4. Adding another 1/2 gives 2 3/4.
Convert the fraction to a percentage: 3/8 = 0.375. Then 0.375 × 100 = 37.5%, which is 37 1/2%.
The time taken was 40 minutes = 2,400 seconds. The distance was 12 km = 12,000 m. Speed = 12,000 ÷ 2,400 = 5 m/s.
Area of a trapezium = 1/2 × (sum of parallel sides) × height. So 252 = 1/2 × (18 + UW) × 12 = 6(18 + UW). Then 18 + UW = 42, so UW = 24 cm.
After giving girls 2/3, the remainder was 1/3. Boys got 5/6 of that remainder, leaving 1/6 of 1/3 = 1/18 of the whole. That 1/18 equals 8 pencils, so the total was 8 × 18 = 144.
Total books = 5,042. First lorry = 3,016. Second lorry = 5,042 − 3,016 = 2,026.
First compute inside brackets: 2/3 − 1/6 = 4/6 − 1/6 = 3/6 = 1/2. Then half of 1/2 is 1/4. Now divide by 1 1/4 = 5/4: 1/4 ÷ 5/4 = 1/4 × 4/5 = 1/5.
Along the length: 120 ÷ 20 = 6 tins. Along the width: 80 ÷ 20 = 4 tins. Along the height: 30 ÷ 15 = 2 layers. Total = 6 × 4 × 2 = 48 tins.
A scale of 1:2000 means 1 cm represents 2,000 cm = 20 m. So actual dimensions are 3 × 20 = 60 m and 2 × 20 = 40 m. Area = 60 × 40 = 2,400 m². Since 1 are = 100 m², the area is 2,400 ÷ 100 = 24 ares.
Use a common denominator of 9: 2x/3 = 6x/9. Then 6x/9 − 4x/9 = 2x/9 = 12. Multiply both sides by 9: 2x = 108, so x = 54.
To round to the nearest ten thousand, look at the thousands digit. In 567,432 the ten-thousands digit is 6 and the thousands digit is 7, so round up to 570,000.
Find the LCM of 12, 24 and 36. The LCM is 72 minutes. So the bells ring together every 72 minutes. 10.00 a.m. − 72 minutes = 8.48 a.m.
Area of a semicircle = 1/2 × πr². So 39.25 = 1/2 × 3.14 × r² = 1.57r². Then r² = 39.25 ÷ 1.57 = 25, so r = 5 cm. Diameter = 2r = 10 cm.
Count the number of days between 27 January 2019 and 18 March 2019. The difference is 50 days, so the pupil spent 50 nights at school.
0.0576 hectares = 0.0576 × 10,000 = 576 m². For a square, side = √576 = 24 m. Perimeter = 4 × 24 = 96 m. Five strands need 5 × 96 = 480 m.
The painted surface is the curved outer surface of the cylinder: area = πdh. So area = (22/7) × 0.7 × 3 = 6.6 m².
Radius = 28 cm. Full volume = πr²h = (22/7) × 28 × 28 × 30 = 73,920 cm³. Half-full means 36,960 cm³. Since 1 litre = 1,000 cm³, water = 36.96 litres.

The circle passes through P, Q and R, so it is the circumcircle. In triangle PQR, angle R = 180° − 40° − 40° = 100°. By the sine rule for a circumcircle, diameter = side opposite angle ÷ sin(angle) = 10 ÷ sin(100°), which is about 10.15 cm. The closest option is 10 cm.
10% of 40 cows is 4, so cows left = 36. Increase goats by 20%: 20% of 55 is 11, so goats become 66. Total animals = 36 + 66 = 102.
For a right triangle, area = 1/2 × base × height. So 54 = 1/2 × 12 × h, which gives h = 9 cm. Hypotenuse = √(12² + 9²) = √225 = 15 cm. Perimeter = 12 + 9 + 15 = 36 cm.
Commission earned = 18,000 − 10,500 = 7,500. Sales above 100,000 = 50,000. Percentage commission = (7,500 ÷ 50,000) × 100 = 15%.
Floor area = 7.5 × 2.5 = 18.75 m². Each tile area = 0.5 × 0.5 = 0.25 m². Number of tiles = 18.75 ÷ 0.25 = 75.
Evaluate brackets first: 3² = 9, so 9 + 4 = 13. Then 7 × 13 = 91. Next 5 × 8 ÷ 2 = 40 ÷ 2 = 20. Finally 91 − 20 + 6 = 77.

The triangle has perpendicular sides 21 cm and 28 cm, so its area = 1/2 × 21 × 28 = 294 cm². Because the right angle is on the semicircle, the third side is the diameter. By Pythagoras, diameter = √(21² + 28²) = 35 cm, so radius = 17.5 cm. Semicircle area = 1/2 × 22/7 × 17.5² = 481.25 cm². Shaded area = 481.25 − 294 = 187.25 cm².
Convert 6 1/4 to an improper fraction: 25/4. Then square it: (25/4)² = 625/16 = 39 1/16.
30 decilitres = 3 litres per day. In 2016, February had 29 days and March had 31 days, so total days = 60. Milk used = 60 × 3 = 180 litres.
A cuboid has 12 edges, with 4 edges of each dimension. Total edge length = 4(l + w + h) = 4(6 + 5 + 4) = 4 × 15 = 60 cm.
Hire purchase price = 24,800 × 1.15 = 28,520. After paying a deposit of 8,520, the balance is 20,000. Divide by 10 instalments: 20,000 ÷ 10 = sh 2,000.

Total mass of 36 pupils = 36 × 45 = 1,620 kg. Total mass of 37 people including the teacher = 37 × 46 = 1,702 kg. Teacher's mass = 1,702 − 1,620 = 82 kg. Difference from average pupil mass = 82 − 45 = 37 kg.
Marked total = 8 × 4,500 = sh 36,000. Actual total paid = sh 34,400. Discount = 36,000 − 34,400 = sh 1,600.

Total cost = 10 × (600 + 30) = sh 6,300. Bottles available for sale = 10 × 24 − 10 = 230 bottles. Revenue = 230 × 30 = sh 6,900. Profit = 6,900 − 6,300 = sh 600. Percentage profit = (600 ÷ 6,300) × 100 = 9 11/21%.

Since RS and TU are parallel, corresponding angles are equal and adjacent angles on a straight line add to 180°. Angle d equals angle h. Angle h and angle g form a straight line, so h + g = 180°. Therefore d + g = 180°.
Cost of trousers = r. Shoes cost twice that = 2r. Shirt costs half of r = 1/2r. Total cost = r + 2r + 1/2r = 3 1/2r. He paid 2x, so balance = 2x − 3 1/2r.

From the chart, Mangoes = 80°, Bananas = 130°, Cabbages = 110°, so Maize = 360° − (80° + 130° + 110°) = 40°. If 40° corresponds to sh 880, then 1° corresponds to sh 22. Bananas = 130° × 22 = sh 2,860.
Every right angled triangle has one angle equal to 90°. The other two angles must add to 90°, so both are less than 90°. Therefore the largest angle is always 90°.
Total work = 18 × 30 = 540 man-days. To finish in 12 days, required men = 540 ÷ 12 = 45. Additional men needed = 45 − 18 = 27.
A rhombus has all sides equal, opposite angles equal, and diagonals that bisect each other. Its angles are not all equal unless it is also a square. So the true properties are (i), (ii) and (iv).
Compare each statement numerically. 48% = 0.48, so 0.45 is not greater. 1/4 = 0.25, so it is not less than 0.2. 3/4 = 0.75, not 0.85. And 7/20 = 0.35, so 0.36 is greater than 0.35.
Angle Q = 180° − 45° − 75° = 60°. Using the sine rule, PR = 8 × sin60° / sin75°. The height RM to base PQ is PR × sin45°, which is approximately 5.07 cm. The nearest option is 5.0 cm.
From the table, departure time from Airport T is 1510 h, which is 3.10 p.m. Arrival was 1.30 p.m. Waiting time = 1 hour 40 minutes.
Brenda has x. Amina has 3x. Together Brenda and Amina have 4x. Juma has 10 less than that, so Juma has 4x − 10. Total books = x + 3x + (4x − 10) = 8x − 10. Therefore the equation is 8x − 10 = 86.

From the bar graph on page 16, the daily amounts are about Monday 15, Tuesday 20, Wednesday 45, Thursday 25, Friday 30, Saturday 35 and Sunday 40 litres. Compare consecutive totals: Tue+Wed = 65, Wed+Thu = 70, Thu+Fri = 55, Fri+Sat = 65, Sat+Sun = 75. The highest is Saturday and Sunday with 75 litres.
